name: Pastrana a Recópolis a Mondejar — circular desde Ambite
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Ambite are characterized by a diverse landscape within the Community of Madrid. The region features the Tajuña Greenway, a repurposed railway line offering mostly flat, red gravel surfaces. Riders can explore the Tajuña Valley, extensive Cornicabra olive groves, and the unique Ambite forest, where riverside, Scots pine, and Mediterranean chaparral ecosystems converge. The Tajuña River also contributes to the area's natural appeal with its green poplar groves. Best mountain bike trails around Ambite The most popular mountain bike trail is Ambite a Estremera a Mondéjar — circular por la Vía Verde del Tajuña, a 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Tajuña Greenway. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tajuña Greenway and 40-Day Train Trail Loop, an easy 13.7 miles (22.0 km) path. This route combines sections of the Tajuña Greenway with other trails, offering varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Pastrana a Recópolis a Mondejar — circular desde Ambite, a 52.1 miles (83.8 km) trail leading through the wider regional landscape, often completed in about 6 hours 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Ambite is defined by the Tajuña Greenway, extensive olive groves, and diverse forest ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging, longer routes. The routes in Ambite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ambite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ambite?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;16 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Ambite, offering a range of options for different skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ambite?
A: Ambite's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain. You'll find mostly flat, red gravel surfaces along the &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, which is a repurposed railway line. Beyond the Greenway, routes traverse rural landscapes with meadows, low scrubland, extensive Cornicabra olive groves, and the unique Ambite forest, where riverside, Scots pine, and Mediterranean chaparral ecosystems converge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Ambite?
A: Yes, Ambite offers several family-friendly options, particularly along the &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;. These trails are generally easy with mostly flat, red gravel surfaces, making them suitable for beginners and families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317079090/tajuna-greenway-and-40-day-train-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway and 40-Day Train Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 22 km path.

Q: What are the best seasons for mountain biking in Ambite?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Ambite are &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these times, the temperatures are pleasant, and the scenery is lush, making for ideal riding conditions. While summer can be hot, and winter cooler, spring and autumn offer the most comfortable experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Ambite?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315861430/ambite-a-estremera-a-mondejar-circular-por-la-via-verde-del-tajuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ambite a Estremera a Mondéjar — circular por la Vía Verde del Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 55.2 km long. For a shorter, easy loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1317056466/fuentenovilla-path-and-ambite-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentenovilla Path and Ambite Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 10.7 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Along the trails, especially the &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Tajuña Valley and extensive Cornicabra olive groves. The Ambite forest offers a unique convergence of riverside, Scots pine, and Mediterranean chaparral ecosystems. You might also encounter historical elements like Civil War-era trenches and industrial ruins. Notable natural monuments include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Ambite's trails?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ambite are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;3.9 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the accessibility of the &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, and the varied terrain that caters to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, the region has several historical points of interest. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; or explore the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; itself also features historical elements such as Civil War-era trenches.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or fountains along the trails?
A: Yes, particularly along the &lt;strong&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, you will find amenities like fountains and cafes in some of the traditional villages that the route passes through. This allows for convenient stops to refuel and refresh during your ride.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route available from Ambite?
A: The longest mountain bike route from Ambite is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314901394/pastrana-a-recopolis-a-mondejar-circular-desde-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana a Recópolis a Mondejar — circular desde Ambite&lt;/a&gt;, which spans &lt;strong&gt;83.8 km&lt;/strong&gt; and typically takes around 6 hours and 53 minutes to complete.

Q: Is there a moderate-difficulty route that explores the wider regional landscape?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316382592/alcarria-villages-loop-from-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcarria Villages Loop from Ambite&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route covering &lt;strong&gt;48.0 km&lt;/strong&gt;. This trail takes you through the broader regional landscape, offering a good balance of challenge and scenic views.
tips: This is a great place to start an adventure. The nature center is a former train station and here you will find maps and information about the area. There are also picnic tables, a bar, parking and a place to rent bicycles.
Palacio de Ambite was built in 1623 by the Marquis of Legarda.
Here you will find a fantastic gravel road perfect for mountain biking or gravel biking. This road runs alongside the Tajuña River between Ambite and the Highway to Olmeda.

In the plain of the Arles river, between the Tagus and the Tajuña, the Villa Ducal de Pastrana welcomes us. Its multiple samples of artistic and cultural heritage have given it the title of Artistic Historical Site.

Pastrana is enriched with figures and literary stories; Thus, its Carmelite convents were founded by Saint Teresa and Saint John of the Cross. Moratín later settled in the town for long periods of time and, closer to our days, Camilo José Cela immortalized it by portraying it in his Viaje a la Alcarria.

The Plaza de la Hora is governed by the imposing Ducal Palace, in Renaissance style and with an abundance of Mannerist elements. Some wonderful coffered ceilings also designed by Alonso de Covarrubias, in Plateresque style, have been preserved. Likewise, the skirting boards of Toledo tiles in the Mudejar style stand out.
It offers its visitors the opportunity to visit various museums and emblematic monuments of the town.
DUCAL PALACE
TAPESTRY MUSEUM
SANTA TERESA V CENTENARY MUSEUM
Recópolis is an archaeological site located near Zorita de los Canes. Here are the ruins of an ancient Visigothic city.

Renowned for its wines with denomination of origin, this town in the interior of Guadalajara has more to offer and is worth visiting. Among the highlights are the ruins of the Convent of San Antonio, one of the first in the Renaissance style in Spain, its Plaza Mayor and the Church of Santa María Magdalena.
